designs.angle: Shippability-first. The smallest real LoopRR is a 3-worker coordinated dev loop on ONE repo with human-in-the-loop at the merge gate, built by repointing Knovo's existing governance spine (governed API + verb-scoped tokens + directive-as-data queue + audit/revisions/soft-delete + routine_runs run-correlation + harness-repo coordination) at dev verbs. The memory moat, Record-and-Replay's deterministic half, multi-tenancy, and cross-repo causal graphs are explicitly LATER phases. I disagree with the brief's instinct to treat the four-type memory engine as load-bearing for v1: for a single-repo loop, GitHub IS the page-store and the git commit DAG IS the temporal backbone, so the v1 "memory" is a thin episodic ledger table plus markdown harness notes — nothing more. Optimize for a loop that closes (request to merged PR with a human gate) in weeks.
designs.thesis: The shortest path to a working autonomous dev loop is to keep Knovo's governance spine intact, swap its content verbs for dev verbs, let GitHub be both the deliverable store and the coordination bus, and gate merge (not publish) behind the existing directive-as-data human approval — deferring memory and multi-tenancy until the single-repo loop demonstrably closes.
designs.product_concept: LoopRR v1 is "a Claude dev pod you point at one GitHub repo." A human (the PM/owner) files a request in the LoopRR HUD (the repurposed Knovo admin control HUD) as a directive: plain-language goal plus a two-axis marker (action x merge_after). Three Claude routine workers — Planner, Implementer, Reviewer — run as stateless ticks coordinated through the governed LoopRR API and a per-project harness repo. The Planner turns the request into a structured ticket (an "artifact" with provenance back to the request). The Implementer claims the ticket, works on a dedicated feature branch in the target repo, and opens a PR. The Reviewer runs tests/review and either flags issues (loop back to Implementer) or marks the PR review-clean. The merge stays human-gated: a merged PR is the new "published" — the API only lets the merge verb fire when an admin clicked approve OR an open directive carries merge_after AND CI is green. The wedge: a solo founder or small team gets a coordinated, audited, recoverable dev loop where every change traces request to ticket to PR, the human steers in natural language without touching the agent prompts, and nothing reaches main without their gate. The journey is: install the GitHub app on one repo, fork the harness, paste three routine prompts, file a request, watch the run-grouped activity feed, approve the PR. The whole product on day one is one repo, one human, one pod.

team_roster.trigger: api (fire-URL from HUD when a request directive is filed) + cron heartbeat (>=1h) for liveness
team_roster.responsibilities: Reads open request directives from the queue, dedups against existing tickets on a stable request key, drafts ONE structured ticket per request with provenance (request id/URL) and a definition-of-done. Never writes code, never touches the target repo. Writes its reasoning to planner/notes/ in the harness.
team_roster.verbs_scope: dedup, create_ticket. (Mirrors Knovo scout=[dedup,create]; smallest verb set because it ingests untrusted request text.)

team_roster.role: Implementer (Knovo Editor repurposed; largest write scope)
team_roster.trigger: api (fire-URL when a ticket is claimable or a reviewer flag loops back) + github-issue/PR event on the harness or target repo
team_roster.responsibilities: Claims a ticket, creates/checks out a dedicated feature branch in the TARGET repo, writes code, pushes commits, opens a PR linked to the ticket, responds to reviewer flags by pushing fixes. Writes run notes to implementer/notes/. Cannot merge.
team_roster.verbs_scope: queue, claim, push_commit, open_pr, comment, transition_status. (Editor-shaped; gains push_commit/open_pr but explicitly NOT merge.)

team_roster.trigger: github (PR opened/updated event on target repo) + cron sweep for stuck PRs
team_roster.responsibilities: On a PR, reads the diff and CI/job logs via GitHub MCP, runs/inspects tests, and either flags concrete issues back to the Implementer (comment + transition to changes_needed) or marks the PR review-clean. Does NOT push fixes to main; flags rather than silently editing — preserves Knovo's Keeper discipline. Writes findings to reviewer/notes/.
team_roster.verbs_scope: review_targets, run_tests, comment, flag, transition_status. (Keeper-shaped: no create, no claim, no merge.)

team_roster.trigger: github push event on harness repo (rebase-before-write) | manual fire-URL
team_roster.responsibilities: Holds the gated merge/deploy verbs and reconciles the harness shared layer (CALENDAR.md, AGENTS.md) from the three workers' notes/. In v1 the HUMAN is the Tech-Lead (clicks approve); promote this to a routine only once the 3-worker loop is proven. Listed here to track, not built in Phase 1.
team_roster.verbs_scope: merge, deploy, reconcile_harness. merge/deploy are the new publish-equivalent verbs, always behind the human directive gate.

designs.coordination_model: Two buses, both already in Knovo. (1) The governed API + directive-as-data queue is the WORK ASSIGNMENT bus: the human files a directive (action x merge_after) on a ticket; workers pull /api/agent/queue (the existing /api/worker/queue, verbatim shape: open actionable directives joined to their artifact) on each tick and act. This is how a PM assigns work without writing prompts. (2) GitHub IS the coordination/state bus: feature branches are the no-race boundary (replacing Knovo's harness 'write only your own subfolder' rule — PRs, not paths, prevent races on shared code). The git commit DAG is the temporal backbone for free; PR state IS the work-item state machine. Each worker run is one stateless tick (Knovo routine model): triggered by cron heartbeat, HUD fire-URL, or a GitHub event; it reads queue + harness + relevant PR/diff/CI via GitHub MCP, does bounded work through verb-scoped API calls, writes append-only notes to its own harness subfolder, ends. routine_runs correlates each dispatch to a Claude session deep link so the human can 'open session' and watch live. Cross-worker handoff is explicit and async: Implementer opening a PR fires the Reviewer's PR-event trigger; Reviewer flagging fires the Implementer's loop-back. No worker ever synchronously calls another worker mid-run (the anti-pattern Knovo already names). Concurrency on the harness shared layer is deferred to the Supervisor phase; in v1 only per-worker notes/ are written by workers and the human owns CALENDAR.md.
designs.memory_engine: DELIBERATELY MINIMAL in v1 — I disagree with the brief that a four-type typed-memory router is load-bearing for the first loop. For a single repo, GitHub already IS GAM's lossless page-store and the git DAG already IS MAGMA's immutable temporal backbone, so v1 memory is two things only: (a) a thin EPISODIC LEDGER — one Postgres table clock_events(id, worker_id, event_type[clock_in|status|clock_out|blocked|handoff], summary, details jsonb, repo, branch, pr_ref, started_at timestamptz, duration interval, ref[SHA/PR/run-id]) with server-stamped UTC timestamps (never trust worker clocks). The synchronous write path is INSERT-only via a hook at task-start/status/task-complete (SemaClaw hooks = clock-in mechanism); nothing else on the critical path. This single table is surfaced as a read-model 'team calendar' and injected into worker context on session start. (b) MARKDOWN harness notes per worker (Knovo's append-only notes/), human-auditable and git-diffable. NO vector DB, NO graph store, NO async LLM consolidation in v1 — retrieval is by-reference (dereference SHA/PR/run via GitHub MCP get_commit/get_file_contents/get_job_logs) plus Postgres FTS/keyword over the ledger and notes. Phase 3 adds the Semantic facts table (Mem0 ADD/UPDATE/DELETE/NOOP with soft-delete via valid_until + superseded_by, mandatory provenance ref) and pgvector hybrid retrieval ONLY once the loop proves a need for durable cross-session facts. The four MIRIX-collapsed types (Core/Procedural/Semantic/Episodic) are the eventual schema, but v1 ships only Episodic-in-Postgres + Core/Procedural-as-markdown. 'Replay' in v1 means strictly Audit/Reconstruct: capture inputs (prompt, injected ledger snapshot, model+params, git SHAs, tool results) per run; we do NOT promise deterministic token regeneration. The DAG-plan deterministic re-execution ('the RR') is a Phase 3 capability, not a v1 claim.
designs.reuse_from_knovo: TRANSFERS AS-IS (the spine, repointed): the governed API as single write boundary (app/api/worker/* -> app/api/agent/*, service-role key server-only, lib/supabase/admin.ts); per-worker verb-scoped bearer tokens (lib/worker-auth.ts — WorkerId becomes planner|implementer|reviewer, the VERBS map becomes dev verbs, constant-time token compare unchanged); audit_log + revisions snapshot-before-write + soft-delete (lib/worker-api.ts audit()/snapshotRevision(), every action audits actor=agent:<role>); the directive-as-data queue (app/api/worker/queue/route.ts shape verbatim — open actionable directives joined to artifact = the agent task inbox); the two-axis human gate (publishAuthorized/editLiveAuthorized -> mergeAuthorized: current===approved OR open directive merge_after, EXTENDED with a CI-green precondition); routine_runs run-correlation (migration 0010, session deep-link, 'Open session' in the HUD); the admin control-HUD UX (docs/admin-hud.md — status markers -> PR/ticket states, directives -> work assignments, run-grouped activity feed -> client-facing audit trail); middleware host-routing (already exists for api.knovo.ai). REPURPOSED: artifact -> ticket (keep zod for ticket SHAPE only, never as a correctness gate — code correctness is CI/tests/review, a probabilistic multi-signal gate, not a 422); source-grounding contract -> request->ticket->PR traceability (sources table -> upstream_refs, UNIQUE(kind,uid) dedup so two workers don't draft the same ticket and a rejected request isn't reopened); the harness repo (foundation/worker-harness.md — constitution + per-worker notes/, but LIFT the 'repo is read-only / workers never write the repo' rule: feature branches replace subfolder-scoping as the no-race boundary); editorial roster -> dev roster (Scout/Editor/Keeper -> Planner/Implementer/Reviewer). DROPPED: the entire molecular kit (lib/renderer/selection.ts PDB grammar, 3Dmol.js, molecular3d stage, explainer renderer — there is no bespoke renderer to protect; GitHub/IDE renders the deliverable); science connectors (bioRxiv/ChEMBL/PubMed/PDB) replaced by GitHub + CI MCP; the narrow-niche invariant (Decision 1/invariant #5 — inverted: the niche is per-project, horizontal is the point); the slot-schema-as-central-artifact-model (kept only as a thin status read-view if at all).
designs.tech_architecture: Same stack as Knovo, minimally extended. Next.js on Vercel hosts the governed LoopRR API (app/api/agent/*) — short request/response only; long coding work runs OFF-Vercel inside the Claude routine sessions (Knovo's existing split holds). Postgres (Supabase) is the system of record for tickets, upstream_refs, audit_log, revisions, comments/directives, routine_runs, and the new clock_events ledger — NOT a vector or graph DB in v1. GitHub is the second store: code truth, the git DAG as temporal backbone, PRs as work-item state, CI job logs as the page-store; the governed API holds the GitHub app installation token server-side and exposes only verb-scoped actions (open_pr, push_commit, merge) — agents never hold GitHub-admin creds, mirroring the service-role discipline. Connectors per worker: GitHub MCP (read: get_commit/get_file_contents/get_job_logs/pull_request_read; write gated through the API), CI status. Triggers per the platform packet: each role is ONE routine carrying combinable triggers (cron heartbeat for liveness, fire-URL for HUD/agent kicks, GitHub PR/issue events for reactive work); each fire is one stateless tick. The merge gate adds a CI-green precondition to publishAuthorized — the API calls GitHub to confirm checks pass before allowing the merge verb. SINGLE-TENANT in v1 (one repo, one human) — multi-tenancy (tenants table, tenant_id on every row, is_tenant_admin RLS rewrite, per-(tenant,role) tokens, usage_tracking 429) is fully designed in Knovo's data-model.md/monetization.md and is Phase 4, gated on the TenantA-cannot-touch-TenantB isolation test. Execution sandboxing + per-tenant CI secret scoping + egress controls (the enlarged blast radius for a code-executing agent) are Phase 4 prerequisites, NOT v1 (v1's single human owns the one repo and accepts that trust boundary).

phase_plan.goal: Fork Knovo; strip the molecular kit; rename app/api/worker/* -> /api/agent/*; redefine WorkerId=planner|implementer|reviewer and the VERBS map for dev verbs; redefine artifact->ticket with upstream_refs; keep audit/revisions/soft-delete/routine_runs/queue verbatim. No GitHub writes yet.
phase_plan.gate: CI green; a ticket can be created/transitioned through the governed API by a verb-scoped token; cross-role verb denials proven by test (planner cannot push_commit, reviewer cannot merge); audit+revision rows written on every mutation.

phase_plan.phase: Phase 1 — Close the loop on ONE repo (week 3-5)
phase_plan.goal: Wire GitHub app (server-side token in the API); add open_pr/push_commit and the mergeAuthorized gate with CI-green precondition. Stand up the 3 routines (Planner/Implementer/Reviewer) against one target repo + a forked harness. Human files a request directive -> Planner tickets -> Implementer branches+PRs -> Reviewer flags/clears -> human approves -> merge.
phase_plan.gate: A single plain-language request reaches a MERGED PR with: traceability (PR->ticket->request), human gate enforced (no merge without approve OR merge_after+CI-green), full audit trail visible in the HUD run-grouped feed, and a reviewer-flag loop-back demonstrated at least once.

phase_plan.goal: Add clock_events ledger table + clock-in/out hooks (server-stamped UTC), surface a team-calendar read-model injected at session start. Promote the Tech-Lead/Supervisor routine: holds merge/deploy verbs (still human-gated) and reconciles the harness shared layer via git pull --rebase on push events.
phase_plan.gate: Every run produces clock_in/clock_out ledger rows with correct duration; the calendar read-model shows who-was-where-when; the Supervisor reconciles harness notes without a write race across two concurrent worker pushes.

phase_plan.goal: Add the Semantic facts table (Mem0 ADD/UPDATE/DELETE/NOOP, soft-delete via valid_until+superseded_by, mandatory provenance ref) behind async consolidation OFF the critical path; add pgvector hybrid (vector+FTS+by-ID) retrieval with SemaClaw degradation fallback. Add Audit/Reconstruct replay (capture inputs+SHAs per run) and deterministic re-execution of the captured DAG plan. Add a 'facts pending review' surface.
phase_plan.gate: A worker can retrieve a durable cross-session fact with verifiable provenance; consolidation never blocks clock-in (measured); a run is reconstructable from captured inputs; no hallucinated fact reaches a worker without provenance.

phase_plan.goal: Add tenants + tenant_id discriminator across all tables; rewrite RLS to is_tenant_admin(tenant_id); per-(tenant,role) tokens; usage_tracking quotas (429) at the API boundary; per-tenant execution sandbox + scoped CI secrets + egress controls; custom domains via middleware. Then cross-repo coordination and (only if proven needed) a graph store for cross-repo causal queries.
phase_plan.gate: TenantA-cannot-read/write-TenantB isolation test passes FIRST and is run in CI; a coding agent cannot exfiltrate secrets or reach disallowed egress from CI; quota caps enforced without starving in-flight work.

biggest_risks: The merge gate is the whole product's safety story and it now depends on an EXTERNAL signal (CI-green) that Knovo's publishAuthorized never modeled. If the API's CI-green check is wrong, stale, or spoofable (e.g. reads a cached check, or a required check isn't actually required in branch protection), a bad PR merges to main. Mitigate: enforce branch protection in GitHub AND the API gate (defense in depth), confirm checks by live API call at merge time, require the specific required-check set, never trust a passed-in status.

Prompt-injection blast radius is larger and LIVE: the Implementer reads untrusted request/issue text AND executes code AND opens PRs that run in CI. In v1 (single trusted human, one owned repo) this is bounded by 'it's your own repo,' but a malicious dependency or injected instruction could still exfiltrate via CI or write hostile code. Mitigate in v1 by keeping the human merge gate absolute and surfacing the full diff; defer real sandboxing/egress controls to Phase 4 but DO NOT onboard a second tenant before they exist.

Unit-of-work mismatch: Knovo's artifact is author-once and self-contained; dev work is multi-step, stateful, dependency-laden, concurrent on the same files. A naive ticket status enum won't model blocked/dependent/partial work. Mitigate: lean on PR state + feature branches as the real state machine in v1 (don't over-build a dependency model); add explicit dependencies only when a real multi-ticket case demands it.

Coordination deadlock / livelock in the flag loop-back: Reviewer flags -> Implementer fixes -> Reviewer re-flags, potentially forever, burning expensive coding sessions. Mitigate: cap loop-back iterations per ticket; after N rounds escalate to the human via a directive ('needs human decision'); meter agent-runs even in v1 to bound cost.

Stateless-tick coordination races: two GitHub events firing near-simultaneously spawn overlapping ticks that both claim the same ticket or both act on one PR. Mitigate: make claim an atomic compare-and-set in the API (claim only if unclaimed); the API is the serialization point, not the workers.

Over-scoping memory too early (the brief's pull): building the four-type typed-memory router, vector store, and async consolidation before the loop even closes would burn the 'weeks not months' budget on infrastructure with no user. Mitigate: hold the line — Episodic-ledger-only in v1; promote Semantic facts strictly on demonstrated need (Phase 3).

Clock/timezone integrity in the ledger across CI runners and routine hosts. Mitigate: server-stamp all timestamps at the API write boundary in UTC; derive duration from server-side start/end events only; never trust worker-supplied time.
what_i_deprioritized: Four-type typed-memory engine (Core/Procedural/Semantic/Episodic with an LLM router) — v1 ships Episodic-in-Postgres + markdown only; full typing is Phase 3 on demonstrated need.
pgvector hybrid retrieval + Mem0-style ADD/UPDATE/DELETE/NOOP fact reconciliation with soft-delete/supersession — Phase 3; v1 retrieves by-reference via GitHub MCP + Postgres FTS.
Async LLM consolidation worker (dual-stream write path) — deferred to Phase 3; v1 has only the synchronous INSERT-only ledger path.
Deterministic DAG-plan re-execution ('the RR' replay) — Phase 3; v1 promises only Audit/Reconstruct, never deterministic token regeneration.

Multi-tenancy (tenants table, tenant_id, is_tenant_admin RLS, per-tenant tokens, usage_tracking quotas, custom domains) — fully designed in Knovo docs, Phase 4, gated on the cross-tenant isolation test running first.
Cross-repo coordination and any graph store for cross-repo causal 'why did A break B' queries — Phase 4+, only if proven a real product need (per the memory packet's own reject list).
Execution sandboxing, per-tenant CI secret scoping, egress controls — Phase 4 prerequisite for the SECOND tenant; v1's single owner accepts the trusted-repo boundary.
Parametric/composed routine prompts (compose(domainTemplate, roleModule, adminParams)) — Knovo's deferred composer; v1 uses three static paste-ready blocks.
The slot-schema single-renderer apparatus and any bespoke explainer/status renderer — dropped to a thin status view at most; GitHub is the render surface.

Client-facing project portal vs private inter-agent forum (the blog/forum + reader_comments repurpose) — both are views over the same audit/comment data; pick after v1, currently the HUD activity feed suffices.
Promoting the Reviewer to auto-open fix PRs (vs Knovo's flag-don't-fix) — kept conservative (flag) in v1; revisit verb scope if clients want auto-fix.

designs.angle: Ledger-first / memory-as-product. LoopRR is not "a dev team that happens to log what it does" — it is a cross-repo memory and replay engine that happens to drive a dev team. The temporal+spatial ledger, record-and-replay, and governed memory writes are the product; the multi-agent dev loop is the highest-value workload that proves the engine. I disagree with the brief's framing that "the dev loop is the vehicle and memory is the moat" only in emphasis: I make the engine the thing you sell (observability/replay/audit of agent fleets) and the dev team the first reference workload, because a memory engine with no workload is a toy and a dev team with no memory is a commodity wrapper. I also harden three places the brief waves at: (1) the second (git) write boundary is governed as strictly as the DB one — branch protection + the merge verb gated on CI-green is non-negotiable from phase 1, not "added later"; (2) "replay" is split into two named product features (Audit/Reconstruct vs Plan-Replay) on day one so we never accidentally promise determinism; (3) the ledger write boundary IS the governed API — every memory/ledger write goes through the same per-worker verb-scoped token path as content writes, giving one audited choke point instead of a side-channel.
designs.thesis: Bet that the durable, defensible asset is a governed cross-repo temporal+spatial ledger with honest record-and-replay — a queryable, auditable "flight recorder + shared brain" for fleets of coding agents — and that a Claude-Code dev dream team is the first workload that makes that engine indispensable rather than a dashboard.
designs.product_concept:
LoopRR is Project-Manager-as-a-Service: a human PM/client steers a small fleet of Claude-Code routine "team members" that plan, implement, review and ship across one or more GitHub repos, coordinated as a single stateful loop. What makes it a product and not a prompt pack is the engine underneath: every agent action is a row in a governed temporal+spatial ledger ("who/what/where/when/why" — worker, event, repo+branch+worktree, server-stamped time+duration, and a ref to the exact SHA/PR/run), and every coordination run is captured as an inspectable plan (the DAG the orchestrator emitted) plus its execution trace, so a run is Reconstructable (Audit mode) and the plan is re-executable (Plan-Replay mode).

User journey: (1) A client connects one or more GitHub repos and files work as natural-language directives in a per-tenant control HUD — the same two-axis directive primitive Knovo already ships (action × publish_after, repurposed to action × merge_after). (2) The Planner/Triager drafts tickets traced to the request; the Tech-Lead sequences them into a DAG; Implementers claim tickets, work on feature branches, open PRs; QA runs tests and flags; nothing merges to main until the human approves OR an open merge_after directive exists AND CI is green. (3) The HUD shows a live, run-grouped activity feed with "Open session" deep links (Knovo's routine_runs), a team calendar/clock-in view (who is in which repo/branch right now and for how long), and a fact ledger ("what the team currently believes is true about this codebase, with provenance"). (4) When something breaks or a client asks "why did we do X," they hit Audit/Reconstruct to replay the captured run, or Plan-Replay to deterministically re-run the captured DAG against current repo state.

The wedge: not "AI writes code" (commoditized) but "AI agents you can trust, audit, replay, and run as a fleet across many repos with shared memory" — sold first as managed dev teams, with the underlying engine (agent observability + cross-repo memory + governed action boundary) as the durable platform other agent workloads plug into later. The temporal/spatial ledger is what a client points at in an incident review; that is the thing competitors can't clone in a weekend.

team_roster.responsibilities: Lowest trust (ingests untrusted client requests / issue text). Drafts tickets from client directives and inbound issues, traces each to an upstream_ref (client_request|issue|spec) deduped on UNIQUE(kind,uid), never re-opens a rejected request. Writes only ticket drafts; cannot claim, branch, or merge. Every action server-stamps a ledger clock-in/clock-out row.

First-class shipped role (promoted from Knovo's design-only Supervisor). Holds the gated merge/deploy verbs. Sequences tickets into the run DAG (the Record half of R&R), reconciles cross-agent state, owns the team CALENDAR/sprint, runs git pull --rebase before writing the shared layer, and is the single actor allowed to fire the merge verb — only when human-approved OR an open merge_after directive exists AND CI is green. Writes the canonical 'team facts' after consolidation review.
team_roster.verbs_scope: plan, sequence, transition_status, request_review, merge, deploy, write_fact

team_roster.responsibilities: Largest write verb set. Claims a ticket (acquires a worktree/branch lease recorded in the ledger as its spatial location), writes code on its own feature branch, pushes commits, opens/updates PRs, comments. Never writes another agent's branch (PR-as-no-race-boundary replaces Knovo's path-scoping). Cannot merge or deploy.
team_roster.verbs_scope: claim, push_commit, open_pr, update_pr, comment, request_review

team_roster.responsibilities: Sweeps PRs and main for test failures, coverage drops, dependency drift, security advisories. Flags rather than silently fixing live code (keeps Knovo's 'flag, don't fix' discipline by default; auto-fix-PR is a per-tenant opt-in that widens its verb scope). Feeds CI/test/coverage signals into the merge precondition. Cannot merge.

The memory engine's slow path. Drains the ledger event queue, runs conservative LLM consolidation to extract Semantic facts and cross-repo relations with mandatory provenance (SHA/PR/run), proposes ADD/UPDATE/DELETE/NOOP against existing facts, and marks contradicted facts superseded (valid_until + superseded_by), never destructive delete. High-impact facts land in a 'facts pending review' surface for the human/Tech-Lead. Holds NO content or merge power — it only writes Semantic memory through the governed API. This role is what makes the moat real and is deliberately separated from anyone who writes code.
team_roster.verbs_scope: read_ledger, propose_fact, supersede_fact (all write_fact-class, no code/merge verbs)

designs.coordination_model:
One stateful loop per project, three coordination layers, all flowing through one governed boundary.

(1) The git layer (deliverable). Each Implementer works in its own worktree on its own feature branch — PR is the no-race boundary (lifting Knovo's harness 'write only your own subfolder' into 'write only your own branch'). main is protected; the only path onto it is the Tech-Lead's merge verb, which the governed API permits only when human-approved OR an open merge_after directive exists AND CI is green (the new precondition that extends Knovo's publishAuthorized). The branch/worktree a worker holds is recorded as its spatial location in the ledger — this is the 'clock-in': claiming work = a SessionStart/task-start hook INSERTs an episodic row (worker, repo, branch, worktree, started_at server-stamped); status posts = tool-post hooks; releasing/finishing = a clock-out row with server-derived duration+outcome.

(2) The ledger/memory layer (coordination + memory). Triggers: each role is one Claude-Code routine carrying combinable triggers per the platform doc — a >=1h heartbeat cron for liveness, a fire-URL for manual/agent/external kicks, and GitHub events for reactive work. A GitHub push/PR event wakes the Tech-Lead, which reconciles (git pull --rebase before any shared-layer write, plus a concurrency group to serialize double-fires). Agents get shared state pull-on-session-start (inject the relevant ledger slice + facts into context) for v1; LISTEN/NOTIFY push is deferred until live coordination is proven necessary. The fire-URL is NEVER the content/merge API and an agent never synchronously blocks on another agent mid-run — coordination is async via the ledger and git events, exactly Knovo's anti-pattern avoidance.

(3) The record-and-replay layer (the spine of trust). The Tech-Lead emits an explicit DAG plan before execution; that plan is stored, diffable, and is the unit of Plan-Replay (deterministic re-execution of the captured graph by a deterministic scheduler — NOT deterministic LLM tokens). Every tick captures its inputs (prompt, injected-memory snapshot, tool results, model id+params, git SHAs, RNG seed where the harness exposes it) so the run is Reconstructable in Audit mode. Two named modes, documented non-guarantee, surfaced as two distinct buttons in the HUD.

Cross-repo coordination is an explicit, permissioned join, never a global pool: a worker on repo A reads repo B's facts only when the tenant has granted that link. Multi-tenant isolation is the same instinct as Knovo's governed boundary: every ledger/fact/ticket row carries tenant_id + repo/org namespace; the API resolves a token to (tenant_id, role) and rejects cross-tenant action.
designs.memory_engine:
System of record = Postgres + git/GitHub. NOT a graph DB, NOT a vector DB as source of truth (I agree hard with the packet here and reject Zep-style and MAGMA-four-graph designs for v1).

Four memory types (collapsing MIRIX's six into dev-native ones), stored as a memory_type enum, not separate databases:
- Core: per-worker persona + standing instructions = SOUL.md/CLAUDE.md committed to the harness repo (git-native, diffable, human-owned).
- Procedural: reusable playbooks (how-to-release, how-to-handle-CI-failure) — also git-committed Markdown.
- Semantic: durable facts about codebases/APIs/decisions/owners — Postgres rows, each with mandatory provenance (source SHA/PR/run), valid_from/valid_until, superseded_by FK, tenant_id+repo namespace, pgvector + FTS index for hybrid retrieval.
- Episodic: THE clock-in ledger — the temporal+spatial backbone.

Ledger table (the moat, in plain SQL): id, tenant_id, worker_id, run_id, event_type (clock_in|status|clock_out|blocked|handoff|plan_emitted), summary, details jsonb, actor, repo, env, branch, worktree (the spatial key — cardinality decision: (repo,branch,worktree) is first-class so multiple workers in one repo are modeled; env is a tag), started_at timestamptz (UTC, server-stamped at the write boundary — never trust worker clocks), duration interval (server-derived from start/end events), ref (SHA|PR#|run-id). This single append-only, immutable-ordered table is the team calendar read-model AND the temporal backbone (the git commit DAG is its lossless extension). Optional TimescaleDB/partitioning ONLY when volume proves it; status events are coalesced/sampled to bound write amplification.

Dual-stream write path (MAGMA/Mem0): fast path = INSERT into ledger + enqueue (pg-boss / LISTEN-NOTIFY / Supabase trigger) — nothing else on the critical path, so clock-in latency is near zero. Slow path = the Librarian/Consolidator role batches recent rows, extracts Semantic facts + relations, runs ADD/UPDATE/DELETE/NOOP reconciliation, soft-supersedes contradictions, and parks high-impact claims in a 'facts pending review' surface.

Retrieval (GAM, adopted wholesale): keep memory LIGHT and lossless-by-reference. Memory stores compact memos + stable refs; a 'researcher' step does hybrid retrieval (three tools, verbatim from GAM/SemaClaw: vector, BM25/keyword — first-class because code identifiers must exact-match, and direct-ID lookup = fetch by SHA/PR#) then dereferences via GitHub MCP (get_commit, get_file_contents, get_job_logs, pull_request_read). Reflection depth capped (GAM default 3) to bound cost. The lossless 'page-store' is git/GitHub itself — we NEVER pre-compress diffs/logs into lossy memory (reject AOT). Three-level degradation fallback (Vector+FTS → FTS → keyword-scan) so retrieval survives an embeddings outage; incremental re-index on write + FTS fallback so a worker can find what another just wrote before embeddings catch up.

Relations live as FKs + a relations join table in Postgres (entity/causal-lite); a real graph store is promoted only if cross-repo causal 'why did PR in A break CI in B' becomes a proven product need — deferred, not built.
designs.reuse_from_knovo:
TRANSFERS as-is (re-target verbs/entities, keep the architecture): the governed API as the single write boundary (app/api/worker/* → /api/agent/*; service-role key server-only, never held by a worker — even more critical now because agents execute code); per-worker verb-scoped bearer tokens (lib/worker-auth.ts's WorkerId→Set<Verb> map, re-typed to dev roles and dev verbs); audit_log + revisions(snapshot-before-write) + soft-delete (lib/worker-api.ts audit()/snapshotRevision(), every row actor=agent:<role>) — this doubles as the client activity feed; the admin control-HUD with two-axis directives and routine_runs session correlation (docs/admin-hud.md, migration 0010, groupActivityIntoRuns) — becomes the per-tenant PM dashboard with 'Open session' live-watch; the multi-tenant GemBlog north star (data-model.md tenants + tenant_id + is_tenant_admin RLS, monetization.md quotas at the write boundary, middleware host-routing) — Knovo's deferred vision becomes LoopRR's day-one spec.

REPURPOSES (mechanism survives, target changes): the publish gate → merge gate — publishAuthorized/editLiveAuthorized in lib/worker-api.ts become mergeAuthorized, EXTENDED with a CI-green precondition (status=published → PR merged/deployed; the two-axis directive action×publish_after → action×merge_after). The Scout/Editor/Keeper/Supervisor archetype → Planner/Implementer/QA/Tech-Lead, with the Supervisor promoted from design-only to first-class. The worker-harness repo design (foundation/worker-harness.md: AGENTS.md constitution, per-worker subfolders, read-anywhere/write-own, single-writer Supervisor reconciling via git pull --rebase, GitHub-push-wakes-Supervisor) — a BETTER fit for a dev team than it ever was for Knovo, with one lift: agents DO write product code, so 'write only your own subfolder' becomes 'write only your own feature branch,' PR (not path) as the no-race boundary. Source-grounding (foundation/content-integrity.md, sources/seen/rejected with UNIQUE(source_db,source_uid)) → requirement traceability: sources → upstream_refs (kind ∈ client_request|spec|issue|design_doc|parent_ticket), keep UNIQUE(kind,uid) + seen/rejected so a request isn't double-ticketed and a won't-do isn't re-opened; the provenance footer becomes 'this PR implements ticket T from request R.' zod kept ONLY for structured metadata (ticket fields, PR descriptors) — never as a correctness gate (code can't be schema-validated; the gate is CI/tests/review). The blog/forum + reader_comments surface → two views over the same audit/comment data: a client-facing project portal AND a private inter-agent work-log/forum.

DROPS: the molecular domain kit (molecular3d stage, 3Dmol.js, lib/renderer/selection.ts PDB grammar, explainer visual stages); the science connectors (bioRxiv/ChEMBL/PubMed MCP, RCSB allowlist) — swapped for GitHub/CI/Slack/Linear connectors, only the per-worker-connector-allowlist PATTERN survives; the single-renderer slot schema as central artifact model (GitHub/IDE/running app is the render surface — keep only the versioning/migrate-on-read discipline for the ticket schema); and the narrow-niche invariant (CLAUDE.md #5 / Decision 1) is DROPPED and inverted — niche becomes per-project, a global wall would kill a horizontal product.
designs.tech_architecture:
Multi-tenant from row zero. Stack inherited from Knovo: Next.js (Vercel) + Supabase Postgres + governed API; extended for long/concurrent/expensive coding workloads Knovo never had.

Write boundary: ONE governed API (/api/agent/*) is the sole path for all DB writes — tickets, ledger rows, facts, directives, PR state mirror. The Supabase service-role key lives only inside it; agents hold per-(tenant,role) verb-scoped bearer tokens with a unique index on (tenant_id, token); the API resolves token→tenant and rejects cross-tenant actions. The SECOND write boundary is git: governed by GitHub branch protection + required reviews + the API's merge verb, kept consistent with the DB audit log (every merge writes an audit row + a ledger clock-out). Two governed boundaries, one audit trail.

Storage choices (opinionated, matching the memory engine): Postgres is system of record for ledger (timestamptz/interval, jsonb, the (repo,branch,worktree) spatial key), Semantic facts (pgvector + tsvector FTS hybrid), tickets, audit/revisions, directives, tenants, usage_tracking. git/GitHub is the lossless page-store for code/diffs/logs/PRs (referenced, never copied). NO vector DB and NO graph DB as system of record in v1; pgvector index only on semantic/episodic text; relations as FK + join table; TimescaleDB and a graph store are both explicitly deferred-until-proven. Secrets in the existing secrets manager (NOT a Knowledge-Vault memory table — reject) — referenced by handle.

Compute model (the real departure from Knovo): Knovo's 'Vercel does only short request/response, heavy work runs off-Vercel in the Claude routine' assumption mostly holds — the heavy coding IS the Claude-Code routine, off our infra. But long/parallel/expensive sessions need: per-tenant isolated execution sandbox + scoped CI secrets + egress controls (a coding agent pulls arbitrary deps — supply-chain + exfil surface Knovo never faced); the consolidation slow path on a queue worker (pg-boss / Supabase edge function), never on the request path; quotas metered at the API boundary (unit = agent-runs and merged-PRs and compute-minutes, returning 429), capped without starving in-flight work.

RLS: is_tenant_admin(tenant_id) rewrite of every policy; the TenantA-cannot-touch-TenantB isolation test is written FIRST and is a release gate. Per-tenant secrets at rest require encryption-at-rest (deferred in Knovo's BACKLOG → REQUIRED here, since clients distrust each other).

phase_plan.phase: P0 — Engine skeleton + the second boundary
phase_plan.goal: Stand up the governed /api/agent/* boundary with per-(tenant,role) verb-scoped tokens; the immutable episodic ledger table (clock-in/out via SessionStart/task hooks, server-stamped time, (repo,branch,worktree) spatial key); audit/revisions/soft-delete carried over verbatim; git boundary governed (branch protection + merge verb with CI-green precondition extending publishAuthorized). One repo, one Implementer + one Tech-Lead, no consolidation yet.
phase_plan.gate: A coding agent can clock-in, open a PR on its own branch, and the Tech-Lead can merge ONLY when human-approved OR merge_after AND CI green; every action is a server-stamped, tenant-scoped ledger+audit row; the TenantA-cannot-read-TenantB RLS test passes. No path to main except the merge verb.

phase_plan.goal: Dual-stream write path (fast INSERT+enqueue / slow Librarian consolidation); Semantic facts with mandatory provenance, valid_until+superseded_by, ADD/UPDATE/DELETE/NOOP reconciliation; hybrid retrieval (vector+BM25+direct-ID) with three-level degradation fallback; GAM-style researcher that dereferences to git/GitHub; 'facts pending review' surface.
phase_plan.gate: A new agent session is correctly primed from injected ledger slice + facts; a contradicted fact is superseded (not deleted) with provenance and is auditable as 'what we believed on date D'; consolidation never touches the clock-in critical path (measured); retrieval still returns fresh exact-match content with embeddings disabled.

phase_plan.goal: Tech-Lead emits an explicit, stored, diffable DAG plan; capture per-tick inputs (prompt, memory snapshot, tool results, model+params, SHAs, seed-if-available); ship two HUD features: Audit/Reconstruct (replay a captured run) and Plan-Replay (deterministic re-exec of the captured DAG by a deterministic scheduler). Document the non-determinism non-guarantee in-product.
phase_plan.gate: A past run is fully reconstructable from captured inputs for an incident review; the captured DAG re-executes deterministically as a graph against current repo state; no surface or doc promises bit-for-bit LLM replay.

phase_plan.phase: P3 — Full team + cross-repo + multi-tenant GA
phase_plan.goal: Add Planner/Triager (issue+api trigger, ticket traceability via upstream_refs) and QA/Maintainer (PR/check trigger, flag-don't-fix). Enable cross-repo facts as explicit permissioned joins. Per-tenant HUD, quotas at the boundary, per-tenant sandbox + scoped CI secrets + egress controls + encryption-at-rest. middleware host-routing per tenant.
phase_plan.gate: Two tenants each steer their own 5-role team across multiple repos with zero cross-tenant leakage (isolation test + security review); cross-repo read requires an explicit grant; quotas return 429 without starving in-flight work; a coding agent's dependency pulls are egress-bounded.

phase_plan.goal: Expose the ledger + R&R + governed action boundary as a product surface for non-dev agent workloads (the engine sold independently of the dev team). Promote graph store / LISTEN-NOTIFY real-time / TimescaleDB ONLY where P0–P3 telemetry proves the need.
phase_plan.gate: At least one workload other than the dev team writes to the ledger through the same governed boundary and is observable/replayable in the HUD, with no new write side-channel introduced.

biggest_risks: Async consolidation writes a wrong/hallucinated fact that an Implementer then bakes into source. This is worse than in chat (a bad 'the deploy command is X' or 'API Y is current' fact gets executed). Mitigation: conservative thresholds, MANDATORY provenance (SHA/PR/run) on every fact, soft-supersession never destructive delete, and a human-visible 'facts pending review' gate for high-impact claims; the Librarian holds no code/merge power so a bad fact can't self-propagate to main.

Cross-tenant / cross-repo leakage is existential for a B2B product hosting clients' proprietary code, and the discriminator does not exist in Knovo today (0001–0011 are single-tenant). Mitigation: tenant_id + repo namespace on every row, is_tenant_admin RLS rewrite, cross-repo reads as explicit permissioned joins (never a global pool), and the TenantA-cannot-touch-TenantB test as a P0 release gate written before any onboarding.

Prompt-injection blast radius is LIVE and larger than Knovo's: a LoopRR agent reads untrusted issue text / client requests / third-party deps AND executes code AND opens PRs that run in CI — injection can mean code execution, supply-chain compromise, or secret exfil via CI. The governed API bounds DB writes but is insufficient alone. Mitigation: per-tenant sandboxed execution, scoped CI secrets, egress controls, and the merge verb gated on CI-green + human/merge_after so injected code can't reach main unreviewed.

'Replay' semantics confusion: engineers expect deterministic re-execution; LLM tokens + drifting CI/GitHub/network state make that impossible. Promising it is a guarantee we can't keep. Mitigation: ship two explicitly named modes (Audit/Reconstruct vs Plan-Replay), document the non-guarantee in-product, and scope 'deterministic' strictly to re-executing the captured DAG graph, not regenerating tokens.

Latency creep onto the clock-in critical path (the exact failure Mem0 pins on Zep). If any consolidation/graph/embedding step sneaks onto the write path, the loop stalls. Mitigation: enforce fast path = INSERT+enqueue ONLY, everything else background; this is a measured P1 gate.

Cost/concurrency blowup: an autonomous dev team runs long, parallel, expensive coding sessions with CI loops — Knovo's one-short-artifact-per-day quota and 'Vercel only does short actions' assumptions don't hold. Mitigation: meter agent-runs+merged-PRs+compute-minutes at the API boundary, 429 without starving in-flight work, heavy work stays in the off-infra Claude routine, consolidation on a queue.

Ledger write amplification: every tool-call status row can explode volume. Mitigation: coalesce/sample status events, server-stamp at the boundary, and reach for TimescaleDB/partitioning only when volume proves it — don't pre-optimize.

Clock/timezone integrity across CI runners and repos: workers report skewed clocks, corrupting the temporal backbone the whole moat rests on. Mitigation: server-stamp all timestamps in UTC at the write boundary, derive duration from server-side start/end events, never trust worker-supplied time.
what_i_deprioritized: MAGMA-style four orthogonal graph stores + beam-search traversal — deferred to a graph store only if cross-repo causal 'why did A break B' becomes a proven product need; v1 uses FK + relations join table. Track as a P4 promotion candidate.
LISTEN/NOTIFY / websocket real-time shared ledger view — v1 is pull-on-session-start; promote to push only when live multi-agent coordination is demonstrably needed. Track.
TimescaleDB / partitioning — deferred until ledger volume proves it; plain Postgres timestamptz table until then. Track on a volume trigger.

Parametric/composable routine prompts (Knovo's deferred composer: prompt = compose(domainTemplate, roleModule, adminParams)) — v1 ships static paste-ready blocks per role; the composer is the path to per-tenant role customization. Track for the platform horizon.
QA auto-fix-PR — default is flag-don't-fix (preserves Knovo's discipline + lowest trust); auto-open-fix-PR is a per-tenant opt-in that widens QA's verb scope. Track as a verb-scope toggle.

RNG-seed / full deterministic input capture — capture what the Claude-Code routine harness actually exposes (confirm model id/params/seed/injected-memory snapshot availability before promising reconstructability); fill gaps as the harness exposes more. Track as a harness-capability dependency.
Encryption-at-rest for per-tenant secrets — required by P3 GA but staged behind the isolation test; deferred in Knovo's BACKLOG, here it is a hard P3 gate, not silently dropped.

The engine-as-platform play (P4: selling the ledger+R&R+governed boundary to non-dev agent workloads) — the strategic upside, deliberately deprioritized behind proving it on the dev-team workload first so the engine ships with a real load-bearing user. Track as the north-star monetization beyond managed dev teams.

Slot-schema single-renderer apparatus — dropped as central model; only the versioning/migrate-on-read discipline is kept for the ticket schema. The universal-core panels could survive as a thin client-portal status view. Track as optional portal polish.

designs.angle: Multi-tenancy + the productized "compose your dream team" experience. I treat LoopRR primarily as a SaaS shape: a self-serve onboarding flow where a tenant composes, tests, and manages a team of Claude-Code routines across one or more GitHub repos, with billing-readiness and tenant isolation as day-one concerns rather than deferred north-star. The lead PM worker runs Phase-0 onboarding as the product's first wow moment. I deliberately subordinate the deepest memory/replay research to whatever is needed to make the team-composition product credible, and I disagree with the brief where it under-weights onboarding and over-weights the memory engine as the wedge.
designs.thesis: LoopRR's wedge is not the memory engine or Record-Replay; it is a self-serve "compose, test, and run a development dream team across your repos in 20 minutes" onboarding that a PM-lead agent drives — and the entire Knovo governance spine (single governed API, verb-scoped tokens, human gate, audit/soft-delete, control HUD) is repurposed to make that team safe to hand real repos to on day one as a multi-tenant SaaS.
designs.product_concept: LoopRR is "Project-Manager-as-a-Service": a tenant signs up, connects a GitHub org/repos via the GitHub App, and a lead PM agent (the "Conductor") runs a Phase-0 onboarding interview — it crawls the connected repos, proposes a starter roster (Conductor + Planner + Implementer + Reviewer + Maintainer), and lets the user accept/edit a team in a visual "Compose your dream team" builder. The core artifact is not an explainer; it is a Team: a named, tenant-scoped set of routine workers, each with a role module, a verb scope, a trigger config (cron heartbeat | fire-URL | GitHub event), a repo/worktree scope, and a memory namespace. The user journey: (1) Connect repos. (2) Conductor interviews + proposes roster. (3) Compose/edit team in the builder. (4) "Dry-run" the team on a sandbox issue (the test step — agents plan and produce a DAG + a draft PR against a throwaway branch, nothing merges) so the user SEES the loop work before trusting it. (5) Go live: file a directive ("ship feature X and merge when CI is green") and watch the team execute in the HUD, which is now a per-tenant PM cockpit showing a live team calendar (the clock-in ledger), per-agent activity, the plan DAG, and gated merge/deploy approvals. The wedge is the onboarding-to-first-green-PR time and the legibility of a multi-agent loop you can steer in plain language — not the depth of the memory substrate, which is invisible plumbing. The mono+multi-repo story is first-class: a Team spans repos, and the "single structured stateful loop" is the cross-repo coordination the HUD makes visible.

team_roster.role: Conductor (PM-lead / Tech-Lead) — Knovo Supervisor promoted to first-class, shipped
team_roster.trigger: combination: cron heartbeat (>=1h liveness sweep) + fire-URL (manual 'run sync now' + Phase-0 onboarding kick) + GitHub event (PR opened/labeled, push to coordination branch)
team_roster.responsibilities: Runs Phase-0 onboarding interview and proposes the roster; owns the cross-repo CALENDAR/sprint plan and the clock-in ledger reconciliation; sequences work, assigns directives to workers, reconciles the shared coordination layer via git pull --rebase (single-writer of shared state). Holds the gated merge/deploy verbs (fire only when human-approved OR an open merge_after directive AND CI green). Writes NO product code.
team_roster.verbs_scope: plan, assign, reconcile, request_review, merge(gated), deploy(gated), comment, ledger_write. NO push_commit/open_pr (cannot author code).

team_roster.role: Planner / Triager (Knovo Scout repurposed; lowest trust)
team_roster.trigger: GitHub event (issue opened/labeled) + fire-URL (Conductor dispatch) + cron (backlog grooming)
team_roster.responsibilities: Reads untrusted issue text / client requests / specs; drafts tickets with upstream_ref traceability; emits the explicit DAG plan (the Record half of Record-Replay) for a directive; dedups against seen/rejected request keys. Smallest verb set because it ingests the most untrusted text.
team_roster.verbs_scope: dedup, create_ticket, plan(emit DAG), comment. NO write to code, NO status transitions beyond draft.

team_roster.role: Implementer (Knovo Editor repurposed; largest write scope)
team_roster.trigger: GitHub event (ticket assigned/branch ready) + fire-URL (Conductor dispatch)
team_roster.responsibilities: Claims a ticket, works in its own feature branch / worktree (the no-race boundary), writes code, pushes commits, opens PRs traced to a ticket. Clocks in/out on the ledger per task. Never merges its own work.
team_roster.verbs_scope: claim, push_commit(own branch only), open_pr, comment, transition_status(ticket), ledger_write. NO merge, NO deploy, NO cross-branch write.

team_roster.role: Reviewer / QA (new role — splits Knovo Keeper's verify discipline)
team_roster.trigger: GitHub event (PR opened/updated, check_suite completed) + fire-URL
team_roster.responsibilities: Reviews open PRs, runs/reads CI + test/coverage signals, posts review comments, flags regressions. Feeds the CI-green + review signal into the merge precondition. Flags rather than silently editing main (keeps Knovo's 'flag, don't fix live' discipline for the protected branch).
team_roster.verbs_scope: read_pr, run_tests, request_changes, approve_review(advisory only), flag, comment, ledger_write. NO merge, NO push to protected branches.

team_roster.trigger: cron (scheduled health sweep) + GitHub event (Dependabot/security advisory)
team_roster.responsibilities: Sweeps for dependency drift, security advisories, flaky tests, stale branches across the tenant's repos; opens fix-tickets (flags), optionally drafts fix-PRs on a feature branch if the tenant opts into auto-fix. Keeps the cross-repo health read-model fresh.
team_roster.verbs_scope: sweep, create_ticket, flag, comment, ledger_write, (opt-in) claim+open_pr for fixes. NO merge.

designs.coordination_model: Two governed write boundaries, both tenant-scoped. (1) The DB boundary: the governed LoopRR API (Knovo's /api/worker/* renamed /api/agent/*) is the only path by which any agent creates tickets, transitions state, posts directives, writes ledger rows, or fires the gated merge/deploy verb. Agents never hold Supabase or GitHub-admin creds. (2) The git boundary: agents write product code only via a tenant-scoped GitHub App installation token, minted server-side by the API per-action with a TTL, scoped to the agent's allowed repos and to its OWN feature branch; protected branches (main) enforce branch-protection + required reviews on GitHub, and the merge verb at the DB boundary is the single human/Conductor-gated path that actually merges. The 'single structured stateful loop' across mono+multi-repo: every agent run is a stateless tick reachable by cron/fire-URL/GitHub-event (per the platform brief); state lives entirely in (a) the Postgres ledger+tickets (cross-repo, tenant-scoped) and (b) git branches/PRs. The Conductor is the single writer of the shared coordination layer (CALENDAR/sprint plan + ledger reconciliation), eliminating write races by construction, exactly as Knovo's Supervisor does for AGENTS.md — but adapted: workers no longer 'write only their own subfolder', they 'write only their own feature branch', with PRs (not paths) as the no-race boundary. Cross-repo work is coordinated through the ledger as the shared event bus: an Implementer in repo A clocking out a task that unblocks repo B writes a handoff event the Conductor folds into the plan. Inter-agent comms repurpose Knovo's directive-comment system: the admin/PM files a directive (action × merge_after) on a ticket; the worker queue endpoint is the agent's task inbox; run/session correlation (routine_runs + RunGroup 'Open session') lets the human watch any agent work live. GitHub push/PR events are the native async event loop; the Conductor must git pull --rebase before writing shared state and uses a concurrency group to serialize.
designs.memory_engine: Postgres + git/GitHub as the system of record; NO graph DB and NO vector DB as source of record (I fully adopt the brief's storage stance). Four dev-native typed memories as a memory_type enum column (not separate DBs), routed by a single lightweight LLM call: Core (per-agent persona/playbook, lives as committed Markdown in the harness repo — user-owned, diffable), Procedural (reusable playbooks), Semantic (durable codebase/API/decision facts with mandatory provenance: every fact row carries source SHA/PR/run-id), Episodic (the clock-in ledger). The ledger is the spine and the product's headline 'team calendar': ledger(id, tenant_id, team_id, agent_id, event_type[clock_in|status|clock_out|blocked|handoff], repo, env, branch, worktree, started_at timestamptz, duration interval, ref, details jsonb) — server-stamped timestamps at the write boundary (never trust agent clocks), UTC, partitioned by tenant. The spatial 'virtual-location' key is (repo, branch, worktree) so worktree-level concurrency (multiple agents in one repo) is first-class — resolving the brief's open question toward the finer key, because multi-tenant multi-agent demands it. Dual-stream write path: clock-in = synchronous INSERT + enqueue (pg-boss / LISTEN-NOTIFY) only; an async consolidation worker does LLM fact extraction off the critical path. Retrieval is GAM-style JIT + lossless-by-reference: memory stores compact memos + stable refs (repo/SHA/PR#/file:line/run-id); a 'researcher' step dereferences to the live diff/log/PR via GitHub MCP tools (get_commit, get_file_contents, get_job_logs, pull_request_read). Hybrid retrieval = pgvector + Postgres FTS + direct-ID lookup, with SemaClaw's degradation fallback (Vector+FTS → FTS → keyword scan) so fresh exact-match identifiers are findable before the embedding index catches up. Fact reconciliation is Mem0-style ADD/UPDATE/DELETE/NOOP with soft-supersession (valid_until + superseded_by, never row drop) so 'what did we believe on date D' is auditable. Record-Replay is scoped honestly into two named product modes: 'Audit/Reconstruct' (capture prompt + injected-memory snapshot + tool results + model/params + git SHAs so a run is reconstructable) and 'Plan-Replay' (deterministic re-execution of the captured DAG plan) — I do NOT promise bit-for-bit LLM replay. CRITICAL multi-tenant adaptation: every memory and ledger row is scoped by (tenant_id, repo); cross-repo reads within a tenant are an explicit permissioned join, never a global pool; cross-TENANT reads are impossible by RLS. All memory writes go through the one governed API boundary with per-(tenant,role) verb-scoped tokens — there is no second memory write path.
designs.reuse_from_knovo: TRANSFERS AS-IS (the governance spine, domain-agnostic): the governed API as sole write boundary (lib/worker-api.ts, app/api/worker/*); per-worker verb-scoped bearer tokens (lib/worker-auth.ts WorkerId→Verb map); audit_log + revisions(snapshot-before-write) + soft-delete; the human-directed gate (publishAuthorized/editLiveAuthorized — the directive two-axis action×publish_after pattern); the control HUD with directives-as-data, the worker queue endpoint as task inbox, and routine_runs run/session correlation (RunGroup 'Open session'). The multi-tenant north star in data-model.md ('Multi-tenant data model' section: tenants table, tenant_id on every table, is_admin→is_tenant_admin(tenant_id) RLS rewrite, per-(tenant,worker) tokens with unique(tenant_id,token), usage_tracking quotas at the API boundary, middleware host-routing) is already drawn — for LoopRR it becomes day-one product, not deferred. The worker-harness design (AGENTS.md constitution + per-agent playbooks + Supervisor single-writer reconciliation, GitHub-push-wakes-Supervisor loop) transfers and fits a dev team BETTER than it fit Knovo. The §7 parametric prompt composer (prompt = compose(domainTemplate, roleModule, adminParams)) becomes the literal engine behind the 'compose your dream team' builder — promoted from deferred to core. REPURPOSES: Scout/Editor/Keeper/Supervisor → Planner/Implementer/Reviewer+Maintainer/Conductor; the blog/forum + reader_comments surface → a client-facing project portal (one view) AND a private inter-agent work-log (second view) over the same audit/ledger/comment data; source-grounding (UNIQUE(source_db,source_uid) dedup, no-source→no-draft, rejected-never-redrafted) → requirement/ticket traceability (upstream_refs UNIQUE(kind,uid); every PR traces to a ticket, every ticket to a request; rejected requests never re-opened); the slot-schema versioning/migrate-on-read DISCIPLINE → the ticket/work-item metadata schema (zod for structured fields only). DROPPED: the molecular domain kit (molecular3d stage, 3Dmol.js, lib/renderer/selection.ts PDB grammar, explainer visual stages); the science connectors (bioRxiv/ChEMBL/PubMed/PDB) replaced by GitHub/CI/Slack/Linear connectors; the narrow-niche invariant (Decision 1/invariant 5) — inverted, the niche is per-project not global; the bespoke single-renderer apparatus (GitHub/IDE is the render surface; keep only a thin status-portal view); and crucially zod-validate-as-SUFFICIENT-gate — code correctness is not schema-checkable, the real gate is CI/tests/review (probabilistic, multi-signal), so the merge precondition extends publishAuthorized with a CI-green check.
designs.tech_architecture: Next.js + Supabase (Postgres) as inherited, re-shaped for multi-tenancy from migration 1. Tenancy: tenants(id, slug, name, plan, limits, domain, domain_verified_at, status); tenant_id NOT NULL on EVERY table (tickets, pull_requests, ledger, memory_facts, audit_log, comments/directives, runs, usage_tracking, agent_configs); RLS everywhere with is_tenant_admin(tenant_id); the FIRST test written is TenantA-cannot-read-TenantB (isolation is existential for a product hosting clients' proprietary code). Per-(tenant, role) agent tokens, unique(tenant_id, token); the governed API resolves a presented bearer to its tenant and rejects any cross-tenant action. GitHub access via a per-tenant GitHub App installation; the API mints short-TTL installation tokens server-side, scoped to the agent's allowed repos + own branch — agents never hold long-lived GitHub creds. Storage choices: Postgres is system-of-record for tickets/ledger/audit/facts; git/GitHub is system-of-record for code (the lossless 'page-store'); pgvector (extension) + Postgres FTS for hybrid retrieval; NO Neo4j, NO standalone vector DB at v1 (entity/causal relations are FKs + a relations join table; promote to a graph store only if cross-repo causal 'why did X break Y' becomes a proven need). Async work: pg-boss / Supabase edge function on LISTEN/NOTIFY for the consolidation queue; the clock-in fast path is INSERT+enqueue only (enforced — no LLM/graph step on the critical path, the exact Zep failure to avoid). Execution model differs sharply from Knovo: agents run long, parallel, expensive coding sessions in the Claude routine harness (off-Vercel); Vercel/API does only short governed request/response. PER-TENANT SANDBOXING is required (not in Knovo's threat model): isolated execution + scoped CI secrets + egress controls bound a coding agent that pulls arbitrary dependencies. Billing-readiness: usage_tracking meters the metering unit (I pick merged-PRs + agent-run-minutes, capped at the API boundary returning 429 quota_exceeded, with in-flight work allowed to drain to avoid corrupting a half-done loop); plan limits on the tenant row; Stripe as a separate later PR. Custom domains via middleware host-routing (already exists for api.knovo.ai) resolving tenant from request host. Secrets: per-tenant secrets at rest for many distrusting clients move encryption-at-rest from 'deferred' (Knovo BACKLOG) to REQUIRED before onboarding the 2nd tenant.

phase_plan.goal: Re-shape the Knovo spine multi-tenant: tenants table, tenant_id on every table, is_tenant_admin RLS, per-(tenant,role) tokens, the governed /api/agent/* boundary with renamed dev verbs, audit/revisions/soft-delete intact. No agents yet.
phase_plan.gate: TenantA-cannot-read/write-TenantB isolation test passes; every API verb rejects cross-tenant + out-of-scope tokens; zero secrets in repo; .env.example lockstep.

phase_plan.phase: P1 — The clock-in ledger + single-tenant single-repo loop
phase_plan.goal: Ship the Episodic ledger (server-stamped, (repo,branch,worktree) spatial key) + the dual-stream write path (sync INSERT+enqueue, async consolidation). Wire ONE Implementer + ONE Conductor on ONE repo: ticket→branch→PR→gated-merge, with CI-green extending the publish gate.
phase_plan.gate: A directive 'implement X and merge when green' produces a PR that merges ONLY when CI green AND (approved OR merge_after); ledger shows clock_in→clock_out with server-stamped duration; merge is impossible without the gate.

phase_plan.phase: P2 — Full roster + cross-repo coordination + memory engine
phase_plan.goal: Add Planner/Reviewer/Maintainer; Conductor as single-writer reconciler of CALENDAR+ledger across multiple repos; Semantic/Procedural memory with provenance + Mem0 reconciliation + GAM JIT dereference; Plan-DAG capture (Record) + Plan-Replay.
phase_plan.gate: A multi-repo directive coordinates >=2 repos through the ledger; a stale fact is superseded not dropped (audit shows valid_until); Plan-Replay re-executes a captured DAG; cross-repo memory read is a permissioned join, never global.

phase_plan.goal: Ship the productized SaaS surface: GitHub App connect, Conductor-led onboarding interview that crawls repos + proposes a roster, the visual team builder (role module × verb scope × trigger × repo scope, powered by the parametric prompt composer), and the sandbox 'dry-run' test step.
phase_plan.gate: A new tenant goes from connect-repos to a passing dry-run (DAG + draft PR on a throwaway branch, nothing merged) self-serve in <20 min; composer's default params reproduce the canonical role blocks (drift test); roster edits are persisted + token-scoped.

phase_plan.goal: usage_tracking metering (merged-PRs + agent-run-minutes) + 429 quota at the API boundary with in-flight drain; per-tenant execution sandbox + scoped CI secrets + egress controls; encryption-at-rest for per-tenant secrets; custom-domain host-routing; Stripe plan flips (separate PR).
phase_plan.gate: Quota cap returns 429 without corrupting in-flight loops; a tenant's CI secrets are unreachable from another tenant's agent; encryption-at-rest verified; a custom domain resolves to the right tenant; plan change flips enforced limits.

biggest_risks: Cross-tenant data leakage is existential and is UNBUILT today (Knovo is single-tenant, no discriminator anywhere). A single missed tenant predicate leaks one client's proprietary codebase to another. Mitigate: tenant_id NOT NULL everywhere + is_tenant_admin RLS + the TenantA/TenantB isolation test as the FIRST test written, gating all onboarding (P0).

Prompt-injection blast radius is larger and LIVE versus Knovo: a LoopRR agent reads untrusted issue text AND executes code AND opens PRs that run in CI, so injection can mean code execution, supply-chain compromise, or secret exfiltration via CI. The governed API helps but is insufficient — needs per-tenant sandboxed execution, scoped CI secrets, egress controls (P4, but partially required before onboarding tenant #2).

Onboarding-to-wow is the actual product risk, not memory depth. If the Conductor's Phase-0 interview + dry-run is slow, confusing, or produces a roster the user doesn't trust, the SaaS dies regardless of how good the loop is. Mitigate: the dry-run sandbox PR (see-it-work-before-trusting-it) is the single most important UX investment; instrument time-to-first-green-PR as the north-star metric.

'Replay' semantics confusion: engineers expect deterministic re-execution; LLM nondeterminism makes that impossible. Mitigate: name the two modes in-product (Audit/Reconstruct vs Plan-Replay), document the non-guarantee, never market 'deterministic replay'.

Async consolidation can write a wrong/hallucinated fact that an Implementer then writes into source. Mitigate: conservative thresholds, mandatory provenance (SHA/PR/run) on every fact, soft-supersession, and a human-visible 'facts pending review' surface for high-impact claims.

Cost/concurrency model inverts: long, parallel, expensive coding sessions versus Knovo's one-short-artifact-a-day. A naive per-PR quota either starves in-flight loops or lets cost run away. Mitigate: meter agent-run-minutes + merged-PRs, cap at the API boundary, but allow in-flight loops to drain rather than killing a half-done branch.

The merge gate must include CI-green, which today's publishAuthorized does not model — shipping the gate without it means the human approves blind. Mitigate: extend the precondition to (approved OR merge_after) AND ci_green, fed by the Reviewer/QA signal, in P1.
what_i_deprioritized: Graph DB for cross-repo causal reasoning ('this PR in repo A broke CI in repo B') — deferred to a later phase behind FKs + a relations join table; promote only when it's a proven product need (tracked, not dropped).
Deep MAGMA-style four-orthogonal-graph memory and beam-search traversal — over-engineered for launch; the temporal graph is just the ledger + commit DAG. Tracked as a v2 retrieval upgrade.
Maintainer auto-fix-PRs — shipped as opt-in only; the default keeps Knovo's 'flag, don't fix live' discipline. The auto-fix verb scope expansion is tracked pending tenant trust signal.

Real-time push/subscribe ledger view (websockets/LISTEN-NOTIFY to the browser) for live multi-agent coordination — P1 uses pull-on-session-start + HUD polling; real-time is tracked for when live-watching becomes a sold feature.
Full Stripe billing integration — metering + quota + 429 ship in P4, but the payment provider integration is a separate high-risk PR (tracked), so plan-flips are stubbed until then.
Enterprise multi-niche / multiple-harness-per-tenant and SLA/audit-export tiers — the data model supports them but they're tracked for an Enterprise milestone after the self-serve wedge is validated.

Retention/GC policy (ledger vs facts vs raw-pointer retention, compliance windows) — tracked as a pre-Enterprise requirement; v1 keeps everything (cheap, audit-friendly) until volume or compliance forces a policy.

scores.notes: Ships the loop honestly and fast, but deliberately defers everything the user named as the moat (ledger, replay, cross-repo, multi-tenant) to Phases 3-4. As written, the v1 deliverable is 'three Claude routines + a governed merge gate on one repo' -- which is a competent wrapper, not a defended product. The author explicitly disagrees with the brief's moat framing and bets governance+shippability is enough. It is not: governance is replicable, and a single-repo human-gated PR loop is the most-cloned shape in the agent-coding market (Devin, OpenHands, Cursor agents, Copilot Workspace all do request->PR-with-human-merge). The honesty about Record-and-Replay (Audit vs Plan-Replay, no determinism promise) is the strongest intellectual contribution and should be grafted into whichever plan wins. Biggest vision miss: the ledger -- the one asset the user calls the moat -- is a 'thin episodic table, Phase 2' afterthought here.

The only design that takes the user's stated moat (ledger + record-and-replay as the product, not exhaust) literally and builds the architecture around it. The Librarian/Consolidator as a code-and-merge-incapable role that only writes Semantic memory is a genuinely sharp safety-and-moat move -- it makes the fact store auditable AND prevents a hallucinated fact from self-propagating to main. The 'engine-as-platform' P4 (sell the ledger+R&R+governed boundary to non-dev agent workloads) is the most defensible long-game and the truest read of 'why can't a competitor clone this in a weekend.' BUT scope realism is the fatal weakness: P0+P1+P2 build the entire memory engine, dual-stream write path, fact reconciliation, hybrid retrieval with degradation, AND record-replay BEFORE a full team or a second tenant exists -- that is months of infra against a workload that hasn't proven it needs durable cross-session facts yet. Risk of building a beautiful flight-recorder for a plane that never flies. The moat is real but front-loaded; needs the lean angle's discipline grafted in.

Correctly identifies that 'compose your dream team' onboarding + the dry-run sandbox PR is the actual product-experience wedge and the literal realization of the parametric composer the Knovo vision already drew (worker-harness.md S7). The dry-run-before-trust UX is the single best go-to-market insight across all three and is a real differentiator in a market where users won't hand a repo to an unproven agent fleet. However the moat claim is the weakest of the three: a self-serve SaaS onboarding flow is a UX moat, not a structural one -- competitors copy onboarding flows fastest of all. Subordinating the memory/ledger ('invisible plumbing') directly contradicts the user's thesis that the ledger IS the moat, so vision_fit takes a hit on the moat axis specifically. Scope is the worst-calibrated: multi-tenancy + RLS + sandboxing + billing + the builder + the full roster is a 6-phase SaaS before a single loop has closed for a single user. Builds the storefront before the product works.

fatal_flaws.flaw: Moat abdication: it explicitly defers the ledger, record-and-replay, cross-repo loop, and multi-tenancy -- i.e. EVERY element the user named as the differentiator -- to Phases 3-4, leaving a v1 that is a governed single-repo request->PR->human-merge loop. That exact shape is the table-stakes commodity of the 2026 agent-coding market (Devin/OpenHands/Copilot Workspace/Cursor). The design wins on shippability but answers 'what stops a competitor cloning this in a weekend' with 'nothing, until Phase 3.' A lean v1 that ships the commodity and only later builds the moat risks never reaching the moat because the commodity attracts no defensible demand to fund the later phases.

The 'GitHub IS the page-store / git DAG IS the temporal backbone, so we need almost no memory' argument quietly dissolves the cross-repo value. Git's DAG is per-repo; the moment work spans repos (a stated vision pillar -- 'cross-repo loop'), there is no native cross-repo temporal/spatial backbone, and the design has deferred the very Postgres ledger that would provide it. So the lean design is structurally single-repo and cannot deliver the cross-repo loop without un-deferring its Phase 2/4 work -- the deferral is load-bearing in a way that breaks the vision.

Inverted build order vs. validation order: it constructs the full memory engine (dual-stream, fact reconciliation, hybrid retrieval+degradation) and record-and-replay in P1/P2 BEFORE the full team and BEFORE a second tenant -- so the moat is built against an unvalidated workload. The packet's own warning ('a memory engine with no workload is a toy') applies to its own plan: P0-P2 is mostly engine, and the dev loop that proves the engine indispensable is not fully assembled until P3. High risk of over-building durable cross-session facts before any user has demonstrated they need them, burning the runway the lean angle would protect.

Async consolidation writing facts that a coding agent then bakes into source is the single highest-consequence failure in the whole product, and while the design names the mitigation (provenance + soft-supersede + Librarian-has-no-merge-power), it still puts the Semantic fact store on the critical path of agent context EARLY (P1) -- before the loop, CI-gate, and human-review surfaces are battle-tested. A wrong 'the deploy command is X' or 'API Y is current' fact retrieved into an Implementer's context produces wrong code that the CI-green gate may not catch (CI passing != semantically correct). The moat (durable facts) is also the largest new attack surface, shipped before the trust scaffolding around it is proven.

Builds the multi-tenant SaaS storefront (tenancy from migration 1, RLS, per-tenant sandboxing, billing-readiness, the visual team builder, 5-role roster) across 5 phases before a single autonomous loop has closed for even one user. The actual product risk it correctly names -- 'onboarding-to-wow' -- presupposes the loop already works and is trustworthy; yet the loop+gate doesn't appear until P1 and the full roster+memory not until P2, while the builder (the headline wedge) is P3. So the differentiating onboarding experience sits on top of an unvalidated loop, and the enormous tenancy/sandbox/billing surface is paid for up front. Highest total scope, latest proof-of-core-value.

Strategic self-contradiction with the user's vision: it explicitly demotes the memory/ledger to 'invisible plumbing' and declares the wedge is onboarding UX -- but the user's stated moat is the ledger. A UX onboarding flow is the least defensible moat layer (fastest to copy), so the design optimizes for the layer most exposed to competition while subordinating the layer the user believes is defensible. It may win first demos and lose the durability argument.

cross_cutting_risks: All three correctly identify that publishAuthorized() has NO CI-green precondition and no second (git) write boundary today -- verified in lib/worker-api.ts. This is the make-or-break safety extension for ALL three and is non-trivial: the merge gate now depends on an EXTERNAL, mutable, potentially-spoofable signal (GitHub check status) that must be confirmed by live API call at merge time AND backed by enforced branch protection. If any design treats 'CI green' as a passed-in boolean rather than a server-verified live check against the required-check set, a bad PR merges to main. This is the single highest-leverage shared risk and none of the three fully specify the spoof-resistance.

The cross-repo loop -- a named vision pillar -- is underspecified in all three beyond 'ledger as shared event bus' and 'permissioned join.' Real cross-repo coordination ('PR in repo A unblocks/breaks repo B') is exactly the case the packet's own reject-list defers a graph store for. So all three promise cross-repo in the pitch but defer the only mechanism (causal graph) that makes cross-repo reasoning real -- leaving cross-repo as little more than 'two repos write to one Postgres ledger.' The vision pillar is more marketing than architecture in every design.

Prompt-injection blast radius is LIVE and larger than Knovo's threat model (agent reads untrusted issue/request text AND executes code AND opens PRs that run in CI -> code exec, supply-chain, secret exfil via CI). All three name sandboxing/egress controls but all three DEFER them to a late phase (P4 in two, Phase 4 in the lean one) while shipping a code-executing agent earlier. Even single-tenant 'it's your own repo' does not bound a malicious dependency or injected instruction exfiltrating secrets through CI. The defer-sandboxing-but-ship-execution ordering is a shared latent vulnerability.

'Forum-as-coordination' (a stated vision element) is treated thinly everywhere -- mapped to 'two views over the same audit/comment data' -- but none designs the forum as an actual coordination mechanism (agents negotiating, claiming, escalating through structured threads). The directive-comment system is reused as a human->agent inbox, not as an inter-agent coordination substrate. If forum-as-coordination is a real pillar, all three under-deliver it; if it's not, the brief should drop it.

Cost/concurrency model inverts Knovo's (one short artifact/day -> long, parallel, expensive coding sessions with CI loops), and the metering unit is unresolved across designs (agent-runs vs merged-PRs vs compute-minutes). The flag-loop livelock (Reviewer flags -> Implementer fixes -> re-flags forever) that the lean design alone calls out is a real runaway-cost vector all three inherit and only one mitigates (iteration cap + human escalation). Grafting that cap into the winner is mandatory.
judges.synthesis_recommendation:
Anchor on memory-moat-first's STRATEGIC THESIS but execute it on lean-loop-first's PHASE DISCIPLINE -- i.e. keep the ledger+record-and-replay as the declared product and the defensibility story, but do NOT build the durable-fact engine before the loop has proven it needs one. Concretely:

ANCHOR (from memory-moat-first): (1) The episodic clock-in ledger is the system-of-record and the headline 'team calendar / flight recorder' from P0, not a Phase-2 afterthought -- this is the one asset that is the moat and the only thing a competitor cannot clone in a weekend, because it accrues value over a tenant's whole history.
